name: Mobile Office Container
description:
Mobile Office Container — Built for an Operation That Moves on Its Own Calendar

A mobile office container is the right specification when your office moves on an operational calendar — every few weeks, every quarter, every season — and the move is driven by your work itself, not by a single project's progress. This page is for buyers planning 4 to 12 unit-level relocations across 12 months, with a 24 to 48-hour offline window between any two operating positions. Single unit, single user, repeated moves.

Five Indian Buyers Whose Operations Are Mobile by Design

Meeting room interior inside a mobile office container.

Five buyer profiles drive this product on Indian operations:

  1. A government circle running rotating field administration — PWD, Forest, Revenue, or election support — across 4 to 8 districts on a quarterly schedule.
  2. A mobile training and certification unit operating six-week deployments at industrial campuses, training centres, and remote project sites.
  3. An audit, assessment, or inspection team taking 4 to 8-week engagements at separate client locations across a financial year.
  4. A mobile clinic administration office supporting health camps and diagnostic drives across rural districts.
  5. An exhibition or seasonal tour office moving between event venues across a touring season.

If instead you need multiple units moving together with one project's advancing work front, our fleet of container offices  is the right configuration. If one unit will be redeployed across projects over a five-year horizon, the specification logic is different. This page is for the unit whose moves are calendar-driven, not project-driven.

Move Cadence Determines the Specification — 4, 8, or 12 Per Year

Stacked mobile office container complex for site administration teams.

Three cadence tiers map to three fitout grades.

Four moves a year — quarterly. Standard container office fitout holds up. Factory-installed AC, surface-mounted accessories, and standard partition walls survive a quarterly move rate without specification changes.

Eight moves a year — every six weeks. Relocation-grade fitout is required. Furniture splits half-fixed and half-detachable. AC mounts on damped brackets rather than rigid welds. Electrical conduit runs surface-routed instead of buried. Partition walls move from screw-only to screw-plus-bracket fixings.

Twelve or more moves a year — monthly or denser. Full mobile-spec build. Every interior fitting is engineered to be packed, transported, and reset within a single weekend's transit window. 4mm commercial-grade vinyl flooring with welded seams. Door hardware rated for repeated heavy use. AC unit removable, not fixed.

Lock your annual cadence at the order stage. The per-unit cost difference between standard and full mobile-spec is approximately Rs 35,000 to Rs 90,000 depending on unit size.

The Friday-to-Monday Cycle — One Move from Decommission to Working Office

Stacked mobile office container complex for site administration teams.

A single relocation runs as a 48-to-60-hour window for one operationally-mobile unit.

Friday afternoon — decommission, 3 to 4 hours. Interior packs into hard cases. AC unit unbolts and packs. MCB panel disconnects at the unit's electrical inlet. Fixed furniture stays bolted. Detachable items move into transit cases.

Saturday morning — load, 6 to 8 hours. Crane-off the levelling pads, load to one 20-ft flatbed. Tarpaulin covers the lifted unit for transit.

Saturday or Sunday — transit. Within one state, 6 to 12 driving hours. Inter-state, 1 to 3 calendar days depending on route and night-driving rules.

Monday morning — recommission, 4 to 6 hours. Crane-on at the new position, levelling pads checked, MCB reconnected, AC remounted, interior unpacked. The office is operational by Monday afternoon.

Total offline: approximately 48 hours, weekend included. Quarterly-cadence operations lose no working day; six-week-cadence operations lose one at most.

Fitout Discipline That Survives Repeated Relocation

Functional workstation layout inside a mobile office container.

Five fitout choices distinguish a unit built for two moves from one built for twenty.

Electrical conduit. Surface-routed PVC conduit on the interior wall, not concealed in panels. Disconnect points stay accessible without dismantling wall finishes.

AC mounting. Wall-frame bracket with rubber dampers absorbs transit vibration. Standard rigid brackets fatigue and crack at the weld point within six to eight unsupported lifts.

Furniture split. Half fixed (desks, document cabinets bolted to the floor), half packed (chairs, monitors, peripherals in transit cases). Fixed items use steel-to-floor bolts, never adhesive.

Partition walls. Screw-fixed to floor and ceiling track at four points minimum. Some flex during transit is acceptable; no structural load is carried.

Vinyl flooring. 4mm commercial-grade with heat-welded seams, replaceable in 200mm panels if a section damages — not stuck-down rolls.

For buyers whose operations may be better served by a lighter cabin-form alternative that loads on a truck without crane hire at every move, our prefab mobile office is the right comparison.

Cost Across Moves and Order-Stage Decisions

A 20-ft mobile office container starts at Rs 3,80,000 in standard fitout and Rs 4,40,000 to Rs 4,70,000 in full mobile-spec fitout. Per-move logistics — single flatbed, two crane lifts, recommissioning labour — run Rs 35,000 to Rs 75,000 within one state and Rs 60,000 to Rs 1,20,000 inter-state. A 12-move year adds Rs 6 to Rs 10 lakh on top of the unit price.

Three order-stage decisions are specific to mobile-spec: cadence-tier (4, 8, or 12+), fitout grade, and electrical configuration (grid-only, grid + DG, or DG-only with surge protection). Each unit is factory-fitted to your specification before dispatch — these cannot be retrofitted cleanly after fabrication.

For the full container office range and category-level procurement guidance, see the hub.

Get a Specification and Price for Your Mobile Office Container

Tell us your expected moves per year, first deployment location, required unit size, and whether your operations are grid-supplied or DG-supplied. We will respond with a unit specification and itemized price within 24 hours.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937

Manufactured at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities. Delivered and erected Pan India in 7 to 21 working days from confirmed order.

Mobile office container deployed on an RCC base for project site operations.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the maximum recommended moves per year before the unit needs structural service?

Twelve to eighteen moves a year is sustainable on relocation-grade or full mobile-spec fitout. We schedule a structural service inspection at the 24-month or 25-move mark, whichever comes first — frame welds, lifting points, and panel-to-frame fixings are inspected and serviced. Standard fitout units are not recommended past eight moves a year.

Single flatbed truck for one move, or multiple vehicles?

A single 20-ft flatbed truck handles one 20-ft unit; a 40-ft unit fits one trailer with an over-length permit. One light vehicle accompanies the truck for the install crew of two to three people. Cranes are hired at each end — one for decommission, one for recommission — at Rs 8,000 to Rs 18,000 per crane lift in most Indian metros.

What permits and how many days for inter-state transit in India?

A standard 20-ft unit fits the ODC-exempt envelope on most national highways and needs no oversized-load permit. A 40-ft unit needs state ODC permits per route, typically processed in 5 to 8 working days. Transit time itself runs 3 to 10 working days depending on destination state and night-driving rules.

Insurance for a unit moved 8 or more times a year?

Standard transit insurance applies per move at approximately 0.4 to 0.6 percent of declared unit value. A structural-fatigue rider added to the annual policy covers cumulative wear from repeated relocation. A 20-ft unit moved twelve times a year typically carries Rs 35,000 to Rs 55,000 in combined annual premium across transit and structural cover.
short_description:
SAMAN Portable
20-ft ISO container · 4 to 12 moves a year

Mobile office container for operations that relocate every few weeks — rotating field administration, mobile training, audit teams, mobile clinics, exhibition tours. Friday-to-Monday move cycle, 48-hour offline target.

Size
20×8 to 40×8 ft
Capacity
1 to 8 people
Frame
IS 2062 Corten
Panel
50mm PUF sandwich
Price
From Rs 3,80,000
Delivery
7 to 21 days
Floor Load
250 kg/sqm
Relocations
12 to 18 per year
Pan India · Bangalore + Greater Noida
+91 97089 89937
